latest.title: Fossils turn old bones into time clues
latest.script_key: fossils can preserve a clue from deep time watch the shape in the rock because fossils form when buried remains leave mineral records behind the original body is gone but the pattern can reveal age habitat and behavior which ancient clue should we read next

latest.title: Bears This scent map gives bears away
latest.script_key: bears can smell a meal long before seeing it watch the nose first because smell drives many bear decisions before the eyes do that huge scent map helps them find food avoid danger and read who crossed the area earlier would you trust your nose that much

latest.title: Ancient trees keep this gothic church standing
latest.script_key: this gothic church leans but that ancient tree won t let it fall see those roots they re not just pretty they grip the stone like fingers in bread dough that s why the walls stay straight i always thought roots were delicate but these claws keep a 200 year old building from toppling

latest.title: Squirrels cheat the tree climb you think you know
latest.script_key: watch those back feet grip the bark like tiny cleats squirrels don t climb with their bellies they push off their hind claws so hard the claws hook into grooves you can t see that s why they vanish straight up trees tiny claws massive power imagine doing that barefoot ever slipped climbing a tree

latest.title: Monkeys wash food when grit gets annoying
latest.script_key: monkeys can change habits to solve small problems watch the hands near the food because some monkeys learn useful routines from their group washing or handling food differently can remove grit and make eating easier the habit spreads because it works which primate trick should come next

latest.title: Ancient tree roots build temples you didn't know
latest.script_key: roots built these temples that zig zag pattern isn t random it s the tree anchoring itself that s why the temple walls lean but never collapse notice how the thickest roots snake around the stone hugging it like a hug you ever walk under a banyan and wonder who s holding up what yeah it s the tree

latest.title: tree bark hides a survival superpower
latest.script_key: bark breathes for the tree see those rough cracks and crevices that s the tree s mouth pores called lenticels pull in oxygen even when the tree s asleep without those cracks a big tree would suffocate under its own bark that s why old trees don t just grow taller they grow wider did you think bark was just armor

latest.title: Trees record hard seasons inside rings
latest.script_key: trees record hard seasons inside rings watch the ring pattern first because fast growth leaves wider bands and stress leaves tighter ones a trunk can store weather history year by year which tree clue should we decode next

latest.title: Trees drink through their bark, not just roots
latest.script_key: bark soaks up water faster than you think see those tiny cracks on this trunk they re breathing pores called lenticels they pull in mist and dew that roots never reach that s why moss grows on trunks above the ground ever touched a tree after rain and felt it s damp higher up that s the bark drinking
